name: DIASTROPHIC DYSPLASIA
definition: DIASTROPHIC DWARFISM = EPIPHYSEAL DYSOSTOSIS severe rhizomelic dwarfism ← generalized disorder of cartilage followed by fibrous scars +

demographics.Genetics: autosomal recessive mutations in diastrophic dysplasia sulfate transporter gene

discriminating_features: severe progressive thoracolumbar kyphoscoliosis (not present at birth) + cervical
narrowed interpedicular space in lumbar spine

Axial skeleton: severe progressive thoracolumbar kyphoscoliosis (not present at birth) + cervical
narrowed interpedicular space in lumbar spine
Extremities: shortening + metaphyseal widening of tubular bones
severe micromelia (predominantly rhizomelic = humerus + femur shorter than distal long
crescent-shaped flattened epiphysis (= retardation of epiphyseal ossification) with
multiple flexion contractures (notably of major joints)
dislocation of one / more large joints (hip, elbow); lateral dislocation of patella
medially bowed metatarsals + abduction of great toes
deformity and shortening of metacarpals + phalanges:
oval + hypoplastic 1st metacarpal bone + abducted proximally positioned thumb =
bizarre carpal bones with supernumerary centers

clinical.Prognosis: death in infancy ← abnormal softening of tracheal cartilage
